message:
test(robot-contract): [7k7pl batch 8] fix cursor/globals pins to match real contract

Refine the cursor + introspect assertions landed in f1833be3 to
match the real robot-contract behavior so the pinned shapes
actually hold across all fixture states.

- cli_robot.rs (introspect_includes_contract_and_globals):
  correct the seeded global flag name from `--data-dir` (which the
  introspect output does NOT carry) to `db` (the real primary
  data-dir option) and add a `verbose` check. Catches both the
  real contract AND verifies the test actually passes — the prior
  upgrade panicked because `--data-dir` is never emitted.

- cli_robot.rs (search_cursor_jsonl_and_compact, both JSONL + compact
  paths): loosen next_cursor from "non-empty string" to
  "string-or-null". The fixture dataset may have fewer than limit
  hits, in which case the cursor is legitimately null (pagination
  exhausted). Prior upgrade failed loud on this valid state.
  The new pin still catches the regressions we care about —
  scalars, arrays, objects, or missing keys — while tolerating
  the null-terminal marker.

Verified green: introspect_includes_contract_and_globals,
search_cursor_jsonl_and_compact, and search_returns_hits_with_
expected_fields all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
